name: streaming-media-engineering
description: Streaming-media (video/audio) engineering team — 3 agents (media-streaming-architect, transcoding-pipeline-engineer, playback-and-delivery-engineer) for the three engines of a streaming build: system architecture (VOD vs live, protocol choice across HLS / MPEG-DASH / CMAF / WebRTC / LL-HLS, packaging + origin/edge design, single vs multi-CDN, DRM strategy across Widevine / FairPlay / PlayReady, ABR-ladder philosophy, cost & scale), transcoding (codec choice across H.264 / HEVC / AV1 / VP9, FFmpeg pipelines, per-title & ABR-ladder encoding, GPU vs CPU, CMAF/fMP4 packaging, captions/subtitles, audio & loudness, transcode-farm scaling), and playback & delivery (player integration across hls.js / dash.js / Shaka / ExoPlayer / AVPlayer, ABR tuning, QoE metrics, low-latency live, client-side DRM, CDN/edge cache tuning, analytics). Ships 4 skills, a decision-tree knowledge bank (4 Mermaid trees + a dated 2026 reference), 5 best-practices, 2 templates, 2 commands. Engineering judgment, not legal/DRM-licensing or content-rights advice; the codec/protocol/CDN/DRM/player landscape is volatile — every version, spec, and QoE target carries a retrieval date + [verify-at-use]; no PII. Requires ravenclaude-core@>=0.7.0.
